skill.name: azure-ai
skill.description: Reference Azure AI services for search, speech, Azure OpenAI, vision, translation, content safety, and document intelligence.
skill.summary: Find Azure AI service guidance, SDK patterns, and MCP tool usage for common AI application tasks.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 195 potential issues, including a critical combined-risk heuristic. Manual review found documentation-only Markdown, SDK examples, and official Microsoft Learn links with no executable scripts, exfiltration behavior, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Formatting Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Backticks and fenced blocks describe Azure MCP commands and SDK install snippets. The files are Markdown references, not executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside Markdown tables or fenced examples. No runtime file, script hook, or dynamic command execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Credential Environment Variables in SDK Examples
low_findings.description: The environment variable references show standard Azure SDK authentication examples. They do not read local secrets for hidden processing or network exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The variables are named service endpoint and key values used directly in documented client constructors. No outbound collector or unrelated network destination appears.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Official Documentation Links Flagged as Network Risk
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are direct Microsoft Learn documentation links. They are user-facing references and do not trigger network calls by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs point to learn.microsoft.com pages in a documentation section. There is no code that fetches or posts data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are keyword collisions in ordinary Azure AI text, such as analysis, linguistic analysis, and caption guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain service descriptions and best practices, not hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Combined Critical Heuristic
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic combines separate documentation signals. Review found no malicious intent, prompt injection, credential theft, or hidden execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The combined signals are legitimate documentation examples and official links. No semantic evidence supports the scanner heuristic as a real threat.

content.value_statement: Developers need quick, accurate guidance across many Azure AI services. This skill maps common tasks to SDK patterns, MCP tools, and service-specific references.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Azure AI Search, Speech, OpenAI, Document Intelligence, Vision, Translation, and Content Safety.
Maps Azure AI Search and Speech tasks to Azure MCP tool commands when MCP is enabled.
Provides condensed SDK references for Python, TypeScript, Java, and .NET examples.
Summarizes vector search, hybrid search, semantic ranking, OCR, transcription, and text-to-speech patterns.
Highlights service-specific authentication notes, client constructors, async patterns, and best practices.
limitations: Requires Azure services, credentials, and permissions configured outside the skill.
Does not provision Azure resources or deploy infrastructure by itself.
Does not replace full Microsoft Learn documentation for advanced service limits.
Some referenced SDK features depend on region, package version, and pricing tier.

use_cases.title: Design Search Experiences
use_cases.description: Plan full-text, vector, hybrid, and semantic search patterns for application search features.

use_cases.title: Automate Document Processing
use_cases.description: Find OCR and form extraction guidance for invoices, PDFs, scanned images, and custom documents.

use_cases.title: Add Voice And Translation Features
use_cases.description: Use Speech and Translator references for transcription, text-to-speech, language detection, and batch translation.

prompt_templates.title: Choose A Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the right Azure AI service for search, transcription, OCR, or translation.

prompt_templates.title: Find SDK Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Show the recommended SDK pattern for Azure AI Search hybrid search in Python.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare credential options for Azure Document Intelligence and Azure OpenAI in production.

prompt_templates.title: Design Multi-Service Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Azure AI workflow that extracts document text, indexes it, and supports semantic search.

output_examples.input: Which Azure AI service should I use for searchable PDF invoices?
output_examples.output: Use Document Intelligence for extraction, then index normalized fields in Azure AI Search. Add semantic ranking if users ask natural language questions.

output_examples.input: How should I approach speech transcription for long recordings?
output_examples.output: Use batch transcription for stored audio, set the locale, enable diarization when speakers matter, and capture timestamps for review workflows.

output_examples.input: What is the safest authentication direction for production?
output_examples.output: Prefer managed identity or DefaultAzureCredential where supported. Use subscription keys only when required by the service or SDK.

best_practices: Prefer managed identity or DefaultAzureCredential when a service supports it.
Use batching, async clients, and retries for high-throughput Azure AI workloads.
Validate service region, model availability, and quota before designing production flows.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for Azure Functions or general Azure resource management.
Do not hardcode service keys in application source files.
Do not skip pagination, confidence checks, or error handling in document and search workflows.

faq.question: What services does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Azure AI Search, Speech, Azure OpenAI, Document Intelligence, Vision, Translation, and Content Safety references.

faq.question: Does it run Azure commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents MCP tool names, CLI references, and SDK examples for the assistant to use when appropriate.

faq.question: Can it create Azure resources?
faq.answer: No. Resource creation, permissions, billing, and deployment remain outside this skill.

faq.question: Which coding languages are referenced?
faq.answer: The included SDK notes cover Python, TypeScript, Java, and .NET where those examples are available.

faq.question: When should I use a different Azure skill?
faq.answer: Use Azure Functions skills for function apps and database-specific skills for PostgreSQL, Kusto, or other databases.

faq.question: Why did the security scanner report high risk?
faq.answer: It treated Markdown examples, environment variable names, and documentation links as executable behavior. Manual review found no confirmed issue.
